context.project_description: Add token usage tracking to monitor LLM token consumption per query across different LLM providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, and Ollama.
key_requirements: Implement token usage tracking for all LLM provider clients (ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, RerankProvider, VisionProvider)
Store and expose token usage metrics with provider-specific tracking for each call
Integrate with existing instrumentation system for benchmarking and monitoring
constraints: Must work with existing provider protocols defined in fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py
Must not break existing functionality of LLM clients including streaming and non-streaming chat calls
Must integrate with current instrumentation hooks in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py
context.existing_context: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py, fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py
stakeholders: user who wants to see LLM token costs for queries

in_scope: token usage tracking for LLM queries

existing_files: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Provider protocols for LLM clients.
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — OpenAI provider wrappers using the official SDK.
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Anthropic provider wrappers using the official SDK.
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Cohere provider wrappers using the official SDK.
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Ollama provider wrappers using direct HTTP calls.
needed_artifacts: fitz_ai/llm/token_tracker.py — tracks and reports LLM token usage per query
fitz_ai/llm/providers/tracked_openai.py — OpenAI provider wrapper with token tracking
fitz_ai/llm/providers/tracked_anthropic.py — Anthropic provider wrapper with token tracking
fitz_ai/llm/providers/tracked_cohere.py — Cohere provider wrapper with token tracking
fitz_ai/llm/providers/tracked_ollama.py — Ollama provider wrapper with token tracking

assumptions.assumption: The analysis is examining a codebase with existing LLM (Large Language Model) integration and configuration files
assumptions.impact: If this assumption is wrong, the token tracking implementation would need to be built from scratch rather than extending existing infrastructure

assumptions.assumption: The file 'fitz_ai/llm/config.py' exists and contains relevant LLM configuration information
assumptions.impact: If this file doesn't exist or doesn't contain the expected content, the analysis approach would need to be completely re-evaluated

assumptions.assumption: The LLM calls are made through a centralized configuration system rather than scattered throughout the codebase
assumptions.impact: If LLM calls are distributed across many files without centralization, token tracking would require more invasive changes to multiple locations

assumptions.assumption: The request for token usage tracking is specifically about tracking costs rather than general metrics
assumptions.impact: If the requirement covers broader metrics (performance, latency, accuracy), the implementation approach would need to expand beyond simple cost tracking

approaches.name: Instrumentation-Based Tracking
approaches.description: Wrap existing LLM provider methods using InstrumentedProxy to capture token usage from API responses.
pros: Leverages existing instrumentation system for consistency
cons: Cannot access raw API response data because providers already strip token metadata during processing
best_for: Systems where provider contracts can be modified to return structured objects with usage fields

approaches.name: HTTP-Level Telemetry Middleware
approaches.description: Introduce a middleware layer that captures raw HTTP responses from LLM providers before they are processed by provider classes.
pros: Does not require changes to existing provider method contracts
cons: Requires deep integration into HTTP client handling within each provider class
best_for: Systems with strict contract requirements and need for non-intrusive telemetry

architecture.reasoning: The Instrumentation-Based Tracking approach is fundamentally flawed because all LLM provider methods convert API responses to simple Python types (str, list[float]) before returning, losing the token usage metadata in the process. This makes it impossible for BenchmarkHook.on_call_end to access raw response data containing usage fields. The HTTP-Level Telemetry Middleware is correct because it addresses this core issue by capturing the raw HTTP responses at a lower level where the token metadata still exists.

key_tradeoffs.instrumentation_vs_modification: Instrumenting existing provider methods to track tokens is impossible without modifying core provider logic, as token metadata is stripped before instrumentation can access it. This tradeoff requires either breaking current contracts or adding middleware that captures raw API responses.

technology_considerations: OpenAI Python SDK and Cohere Python SDK both convert API responses to simple types (str/list[float]) before returning them, making token metadata inaccessible for instrumentation
InstrumentedProxy._wrap_method operates on already-processed results rather than raw HTTP responses, so it cannot extract usage data that was stripped during provider method execution
HTTP-level telemetry tools like mitmproxy or custom HTTP interceptors are needed to capture raw API response bodies before providers parse them into simple types
Wrapper factory patterns must be introduced at the provider instantiation level to properly instrument methods with token tracking, rather than relying on post-processing wrappers
Context management is required for aggregating token usage across multiple LLM interactions within a single query
architecture.scope_statement: Implement a telemetry middleware layer that intercepts raw HTTP requests/responses from LLM providers to extract and aggregate token usage data, requiring modifications to provider classes to capture responses before processing.

adrs.title: ADR: Token Usage Tracking Requires Deep Integration, Not Simple Instrumentation
adrs.context: The architectural reasoning claimed token usage tracking could be implemented by wrapping existing LLM provider methods using `InstrumentedProxy`, but this contradicts actual source code behavior.
adrs.decision: Token usage tracking cannot be implemented via simple method wrapping because all current providers convert API responses to simple types (strings, lists) before returning them, stripping away token metadata. Instead, we must modify providers to preserve or expose token information or instrument at the HTTP client layer.
adrs.rationale: The actual LLM provider implementations return only content data - strings for chat and lists of floats for embeddings - with no access to raw API response objects that contain usage fields like token counts. This means there's nothing for BenchmarkHook.on_call_end to extract from, nor can InstrumentedProxy._wrap_method access the original metadata because it's already been consumed by provider methods.
consequences: Simple instrumentation approach fails and leads to incorrect assumptions about data flow
Breaking change required if we want to maintain current API contracts while adding token tracking
New architecture needed for capturing raw HTTP responses before processing in providers
Provider factories must be updated to return instrumented versions
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1: Modify provider methods to return structured objects containing both content and usage data – rejected because it breaks existing API contracts
Alternative 2: Instrument at the HTTP client layer instead of method level – rejected because requires significant refactoring of all LLM providers
Alternative 3: Add middleware that captures raw JSON before processing in each provider – rejected due to complexity and potential performance impact

adrs.title: ADR: Raw HTTP Response Data Exposure vs. Security/Privacy
adrs.context: The HTTP-Level Telemetry Middleware captures and propagates raw HTTP responses including token usage metadata across service boundaries, which could expose sensitive information or violate data privacy constraints.
adrs.decision: Accept exposure of raw HTTP response data as necessary for accurate telemetry, with explicit opt-in mechanisms for services that need to restrict this data flow
adrs.rationale: The core value proposition requires access to token usage and other metadata embedded in raw API responses. However, this creates a security boundary crossing where sensitive data (including potentially PII in some LLM responses) flows through the telemetry system. The tradeoff is between complete observability and strict data isolation
consequences: Services must explicitly opt-in to receive raw response data for telemetry purposes
Additional audit logging required when raw HTTP responses flow across service boundaries
Potential compliance concerns with data residency requirements that restrict cross-service data flows
alternatives_considered: Only propagate structured metadata fields rather than raw responses (reduces observability but increases security isolation)
Implement a separate secure telemetry channel for sensitive response data (adds complexity and latency)

artifacts.purpose: OpenAI provider wrappers using the official SDK.
Anthropic provider wrappers using the official SDK.

artifacts.content:
from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Any, Iterator

from fitz_ai.llm.auth import AuthProvider
from fitz_ai.llm.providers.base import ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, RerankProvider
from fitz_ai.llm.types import ModelTier
import cohere
import httpx
import logging

CHAT_MODELS = {
    ModelTier.FAST: "command-r-plus",
    ModelTier.BALANCED: "command-r-plus",
    ModelTier.SLOW: "command-r-plus",
}

EMBEDDING_MODEL = "embed-english-v3.0"
RERANK_MODEL = "rerank-multilingual-v2.0"

class CohereChat(ChatProvider):
    def __init__(self, auth: AuthProvider, model: str | None, tier: ModelTier, models: dict[ModelTier, str] | None):
        self.auth = auth
        self.model = model or CHAT_MODELS[tier]
        self.client = cohere.Client(
            api_key=auth.get_headers()["Authorization"].replace("Bearer ", ""),
            http_client=httpx.Client(),
        )

    def chat(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:
        response = self.client.chat(
            model=self.model,
            message=messages[-1]["content"] if messages else "",
            preamble=messages[0]["content"] if messages and messages[0]["role"] == "system" else None
        )
        return response.text

    def chat_stream(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> Iterator[str]:
        stream = self.client.chat_stream(
            model=self.model,
            message=messages[-1]["content"] if messages else "",
            preamble=messages[0]["content"] if messages and messages[0]["role"] == "system" else None
        )
        for chunk in stream:
            if hasattr(chunk, 'text') and chunk.text is not None:
                yield chunk.text

class CohereEmbedding(EmbeddingProvider):
    def __init__(self, auth: AuthProvider, model: str | None, input_type: str, dimensions: int | None):
        self.auth = auth
        self.model = model or EMBEDDING_MODEL
        self.input_type = input_type
        self.dimensions = dimensions
        self.client = cohere.Client(
            api_key=auth.get_headers()["Authorization"].replace("Bearer ", ""),
            http_client=httpx.Client(),
        )

    def embed(self, text: str) -> list[float]:
        response = self.client.embed(
            texts=[text],
            model=self.model,
            input_type=self.input_type,
            embedding_types=["float"]
        )
        return response.embeddings[0]

    def embed_batch(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
        response = self.client.embed(
            texts=texts,
            model=self.model,
            input_type=self.input_type,
            embedding_types=["float"]
        )
        return response.embeddings

    def _embed_single_batch(self, texts: list[str], input_type: str | None) -> list[list[float]]:
        response = self.client.embed(
            texts=texts,
            model=self.model,
            input_type=input_type or self.input_type,
            embedding_types=["float"]
        )
        return response.embeddings

    def dimensions(self) -> int:
        return 1024 if not self.dimensions else self.dimensions

class CohereRerank(RerankProvider):
    def __init__(self, auth: AuthProvider, model: str | None):
        self.auth = auth
        self.model = model or RERANK_MODEL
        self.client = cohere.Client(
            api_key=auth.get_headers()["Authorization"].replace("Bearer ", ""),
            http_client=httpx.Client(),
        )

    def rerank(self, query: str, documents: list[str], top_n: int | None) -> list[RerankResult]:
        response = self.client.rerank(
            model=self.model,
            query=query,
            documents=documents,
            top_n=top_n
        )
        results = []
        for result in response.results:
            # Assuming RerankResult has score and index attributes
            rerank_result = RerankResult(score=result.relevance_score, index=result.index)
            results.append(rerank_result)
        return results

artifacts.content:
from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Any, Iterator

from fitz_ai.llm.auth import AuthProvider
from fitz_ai.llm.providers.base import ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, RerankProvider
from fitz_ai.llm.types import ModelTier
import cohere
import httpx
import logging

CHAT_MODELS = {
    ModelTier.FAST: "command-r-plus",
    ModelTier.BALANCED: "command-r-plus",
    ModelTier.SLOW: "command-r-plus",
}

EMBEDDING_MODEL = "embed-english-v3.0"
RERANK_MODEL = "rerank-multilingual-v2.0"

class CohereChat(ChatProvider):
    def __init__(self, auth: AuthProvider, model: str | None, tier: ModelTier, models: dict[ModelTier, str] | None):
        self.auth = auth
        self.model = model or CHAT_MODELS[tier]
        self.client = cohere.Client(
            api_key=auth.get_headers()["Authorization"].replace("Bearer ", ""),
            http_client=httpx.Client(),
        )

    def chat(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:
        response = self.client.chat(
            model=self.model,
            message=messages[-1]["content"] if messages else "",
            preamble=messages[0]["content"] if messages and messages[0]["role"] == "system" else None
        )
        return response.text

    def chat_stream(self, messages: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> Iterator[str]:
        stream = self.client.chat_stream(
            model=self.model,
            message=messages[-1]["content"] if messages else "",
            preamble=messages[0]["content"] if messages and messages[0]["role"] == "system" else None
        )
        for chunk in stream:
            if hasattr(chunk, 'text') and chunk.text is not None:
                yield chunk.text

class CohereEmbedding(EmbeddingProvider):
    def __init__(self, auth: AuthProvider, model: str | None, input_type: str, dimensions: int | None):
        self.auth = auth
        self.model = model or EMBEDDING_MODEL
        self.input_type = input_type
        self.dimensions = dimensions
        self.client = cohere.Client(
            api_key=auth.get_headers()["Authorization"].replace("Bearer ", ""),
            http_client=httpx.Client(),
        )

    def embed(self, text: str) -> list[float]:
        response = self.client.embed(
            texts=[text],
            model=self.model,
            input_type=self.input_type,
            embedding_types=["float"]
        )
        return response.embeddings[0]

    def embed_batch(self, texts: list[str]) -> list[list[float]]:
        response = self.client.embed(
            texts=texts,
            model=self.model,
            input_type=self.input_type,
            embedding_types=["float"]
        )
        return response.embeddings

    def _embed_single_batch(self, texts: list[str], input_type: str | None) -> list[list[float]]:
        response = self.client.embed(
            texts=texts,
            model=self.model,
            input_type=input_type or self.input_type,
            embedding_types=["float"]
        )
        return response.embeddings

    def dimensions(self) -> int:
        return 1024 if not self.dimensions else self.dimensions

class CohereRerank(RerankProvider):
    def __init__(self, auth: AuthProvider, model: str | None):
        self.auth = auth
        self.model = model or RERANK_MODEL
        self.client = cohere.Client(
            api_key=auth.get_headers()["Authorization"].replace("Bearer ", ""),
            http_client=httpx.Client(),
        )

    def rerank(self, query: str, documents: list[str], top_n: int | None) -> list[RerankResult]:
        response = self.client.rerank(
            model=self.model,
            query=query,
            documents=documents,
            top_n=top_n
        )
        results = []
        for result in response.results:
            # Assuming RerankResult has score and index attributes
            rerank_result = RerankResult(score=result.relevance_score, index=result.index)
            results.append(rerank_result)
        return results

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
phases.verification_command: pytest tests/llm/providers/test_base.py tests/llm/providers/test_openai.py tests/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py tests/llm/providers/test_cohere.py tests/llm/providers/test_ollama.py -v

phases.name: Core Token Usage Tracking Infrastructure
phases.objective: Create data structures and HTTP-level middleware to capture LLM token usage from API responses
key_risks: HTTP-level middleware may not capture all API responses properly, especially streaming cases
Performance overhead from telemetry middleware could impact LLM processing speed
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.token_tracker import TokenUsage; from fitz_ai.llm.http_telemetry import TelemetryMiddleware; print('Token tracking infrastructure imports successfully')"

phases.name: OpenAI Token Tracking Implementation
phases.objective: Implement tracked versions of OpenAI providers with token usage tracking capability
key_risks: Integration with existing OpenAI provider classes may introduce compatibility issues
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.providers.tracked_openai import TrackedOpenAIChat, TrackedOpenAIEmbedding; print('OpenAI tracked providers imported successfully')"

phases.name: Extend to Other Providers
phases.objective: Implement tracked versions for Anthropic, Cohere and Ollama providers with token usage tracking capability
key_risks: Provider-specific response format parsing may break if API schema changes
HTTP transport compatibility issues with different LLM SDKs or direct HTTP clients
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.providers.tracked_anthropic import TrackedAnthropicChat; from fitz_ai.llm.providers.tracked_cohere import TrackedCohereEmbedding, TrackedCohereRerank; from fitz_ai.llm.providers.tracked_ollama import TrackedOllamaChat, TrackedOllamaEmbedding, TrackedOllamaRerank; print('All tracked providers imported successfully')"

phases.name: Integration and API Exposure
phases.objective: Integrate token tracking into FitzKragEngine and expose APIs for accessing token usage statistics
deliverables: Configuration updates in fitz_ai/llm/config.py to use tracked providers by default
API functions get_current_token_usage() and reset_token_usage() in fitz_ai/llm/token_tracker.py
key_risks: Instrumentation hook approach may interfere with existing benchmarking systems
Token tracking APIs could expose sensitive information or be accessible where they shouldn't be
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.token_tracker import TokenUsage, get_current_token_usage, reset_token_usage; from fitz_ai.engines.fitz_krag.engine import FitzKragEngine, _get_engine_tokens; print('Token tracking APIs imported successfully')"

phases.name: Testing and Documentation
phases.objective: Add unit tests for token tracking functionality and verify all components work together correctly
deliverables: Unit tests in tests/unit/test_token_tracking.py covering TokenUsage and TelemetryMiddleware classes
key_risks: Tests may not cover all edge cases in streaming response handling or API format changes
Integration testing across all providers and scenarios might reveal unexpected issues

risks.description: HTTP-level telemetry middleware may not integrate cleanly with existing SDK clients or may introduce performance overhead
risks.mitigation: Conduct load tests on simple chat completion calls to ensure no regression in latency and verify compatibility with all provider SDKs
risks.contingency: Fall back to alternative instrumentation methods if middleware integration fails, or implement performance monitoring to detect regressions
risks.verification: Run comprehensive load tests and verify that token tracking doesn't significantly impact response times

risks.description: Streaming responses may not expose complete token metadata until stream completion, preventing real-time tracking during long-running generation
risks.mitigation: Implement streaming-specific handling with partial tracking capabilities and logging for incomplete data
risks.contingency: Provide warning logs when token usage is only available after stream completion rather than in real-time
risks.verification: Test actual streaming scenarios with OpenAI/Anthropic to ensure tokens are captured at appropriate intervals

risks.description: Provider-specific API response format changes could break token parsing logic silently or inaccurately
risks.mitigation: Add fallback mechanisms and graceful degradation in parsers to handle missing fields gracefully
risks.contingency: Implement comprehensive logging for parsing failures with alerting mechanism to notify when parsing stops working
risks.verification: Add unit tests that cover various response formats and implement fallback strategies in the parser logic

risks.description: LLM providers may change their API response schemas which would break our parsing logic without notice
risks.mitigation: Implement robust error handling and logging to detect broken token extraction immediately when it happens
risks.contingency: Create periodic monitoring script that tests token extraction against sample API responses from each provider
risks.verification: Add comprehensive unit test coverage for parsing different response formats and implement error logging for missing fields

risks.description: Raw HTTP response exposure in middleware could potentially contain sensitive data that might leak through telemetry infrastructure
risks.mitigation: Ensure no logging of raw API response bodies, only parse and log the necessary metadata fields we need for token tracking
risks.contingency: Implement strict access controls on any telemetry data access points and review all logging output to exclude sensitive information
risks.verification: Review all middleware logging calls and ensure they only log metadata fields, not the full response body

risks.description: Instrumentation hook approach may conflict with existing hook systems used elsewhere in codebase
risks.mitigation: Run comprehensive test suite after integration to verify hooks don't interfere with each other
risks.contingency: Implement alternative tracking mechanism if BenchmarkHook conflicts are detected, such as using a custom metrics collection approach
risks.verification: Execute full test suite including existing instrumentation tests to confirm no hook interference

risks.description: Token metadata may not be available for all LLM APIs at specific times or under certain conditions, leading to missing data
risks.mitigation: Implement detailed logging that warns when token parsing fails and maintain comprehensive fallback strategies
risks.contingency: Add alerting mechanisms in monitoring systems for cases where token usage tracking is not available
risks.verification: Create test scenarios that simulate API responses missing token metadata to ensure proper error handling

recommended_contingencies: Implement comprehensive logging and alerting for parsing failures or missing token data so issues can be detected immediately
Add performance monitoring and benchmarking tests to catch any regressions in LLM processing speed due to telemetry middleware overhead
Create a periodic test suite that validates token extraction against sample API responses from each supported provider to detect breaking changes early

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
phases.verification_command: pytest tests/llm/providers/test_base.py tests/llm/providers/test_openai.py tests/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py tests/llm/providers/test_cohere.py tests/llm/providers/test_ollama.py -v

phases.name: Core Token Usage Tracking Infrastructure
phases.objective: Create data structures and HTTP-level middleware to capture LLM token usage from API responses
key_risks: HTTP-level middleware may not capture all API responses properly, especially streaming cases
Performance overhead from telemetry middleware could impact LLM processing speed
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.token_tracker import TokenUsage; from fitz_ai.llm.http_telemetry import TelemetryMiddleware; print('Token tracking infrastructure imports successfully')"

phases.name: OpenAI Token Tracking Implementation
phases.objective: Implement tracked versions of OpenAI providers with token usage tracking capability
key_risks: Integration with existing OpenAI provider classes may introduce compatibility issues
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.providers.tracked_openai import TrackedOpenAIChat, TrackedOpenAIEmbedding; print('OpenAI tracked providers imported successfully')"

phases.name: Extend to Other Providers
phases.objective: Implement tracked versions for Anthropic, Cohere and Ollama providers with token usage tracking capability
key_risks: Provider-specific response format parsing may break if API schema changes
HTTP transport compatibility issues with different LLM SDKs or direct HTTP clients
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.providers.tracked_anthropic import TrackedAnthropicChat; from fitz_ai.llm.providers.tracked_cohere import TrackedCohereEmbedding, TrackedCohereRerank; from fitz_ai.llm.providers.tracked_ollama import TrackedOllamaChat, TrackedOllamaEmbedding, TrackedOllamaRerank; print('All tracked providers imported successfully')"

phases.name: Integration and API Exposure
phases.objective: Integrate token tracking into FitzKragEngine and expose APIs for accessing token usage statistics
deliverables: Configuration updates in fitz_ai/llm/config.py to use tracked providers by default
API functions get_current_token_usage() and reset_token_usage() in fitz_ai/llm/token_tracker.py
key_risks: Instrumentation hook approach may interfere with existing benchmarking systems
Token tracking APIs could expose sensitive information or be accessible where they shouldn't be
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.llm.token_tracker import TokenUsage, get_current_token_usage, reset_token_usage; from fitz_ai.engines.fitz_krag.engine import FitzKragEngine, _get_engine_tokens; print('Token tracking APIs imported successfully')"

phases.name: Testing and Documentation
phases.objective: Add unit tests for token tracking functionality and verify all components work together correctly
deliverables: Unit tests in tests/unit/test_token_tracking.py covering TokenUsage and TelemetryMiddleware classes
key_risks: Tests may not cover all edge cases in streaming response handling or API format changes
Integration testing across all providers and scenarios might reveal unexpected issues

risks.description: HTTP-level telemetry middleware may not integrate cleanly with existing SDK clients or may introduce performance overhead
risks.mitigation: Conduct load tests on simple chat completion calls to ensure no regression in latency and verify compatibility with all provider SDKs
risks.contingency: Fall back to alternative instrumentation methods if middleware integration fails, or implement performance monitoring to detect regressions
risks.verification: Run comprehensive load tests and verify that token tracking doesn't significantly impact response times
